title: Christmas Box Office Upset: Marty Supreme Beats Anaconda, Avatar 3 Crossing $700M Globally
text: Sony's comedy-action film Anaconda opened during the Christmas holiday weekend with a four-day opening projection of $22 million from 3,509 theaters. The film stars Jack Black and Paul Rudd alongside a giant CG serpent. Director Tom Gormican's meta-comedy follows a movie director and crew traveling to the Amazon to create a definitive film about the legendary monster creature. Critics were less enthusiastic than audiences. The film currently holds a 51% critics score on Rotten Tomatoes, while viewers gave it a B CinemaScore and a 78% audience rating on the same platform. An action-comedy starring Jack Black, Paul Rudd and a giant CG snake should be way more fun. The cast also includes Thandiwe Newton and Steve Zahn. Surprisingly, Anaconda finished behind A24's Marty Supreme (starring Timothée Chalamet) for the holiday weekend's second-place position. Avatar: Fire and Ash dominated the box office overall, earning $80-85 million in its second weekend.
